# descope_policy_rule (Resource)

Manages a declarative Descope policy rule through the Management Service policy-rule CRUD API.

## Schema

### Required

- `action_kinds` (List of String) The action kinds matched by the rule. Supported values are `user_access`, `client_access`, `exchange_token`, and `fetch_outbound_token`.
- `effect` (String) The rule effect. Must be `permit` or `forbid`; `forbid` may require a project feature flag.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the policy rule participates in authorization decisions.
- `name` (String) A name for the policy rule.
- `principal_type` (String) The principal type matched by the rule. Must be `any`, `user`, or `client`; `user` may require a project feature flag.
- `project_id` (String) The ID of the Descope project this policy rule belongs to. Changing this value requires replacement.
- `rule_family` (String) The policy rule family. Must be `resource_access`, `outbound_access`, or `token_exchange`.

### Optional

- `conditions` (Attributes List) Structured conditions that are combined with logical AND. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--conditions))
- `description` (String) A description for the policy rule.
- `grants` (Attributes List) The scopes and audiences granted by a permit rule. Token-exchange permit rules require at least one grant. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--grants))
- `principal_selector` (List of String) Optional principal IDs matched by the rule. An empty list matches any principal of the selected type.
- `resource_targets` (Attributes List) The resource targets matched by the rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--resource_targets))

### Read-Only

- `cedar_text` (String) The Cedar policy source generated and validated by Descope. This value is read-only.
- `created_time` (Number) The rule creation time as a Unix timestamp. This value is read-only.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `modified_time` (Number) The rule modification time as a Unix timestamp. This value is read-only.
- `version` (Number) The server-managed policy rule version used for optimistic concurrency. This value is read-only.

<a id="nestedatt--conditions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `conditions`

Required:

- `key` (String) The policy context key evaluated by this condition.
- `operator` (String) The comparison operator. Must be `equal`, `notEqual`, `contains`, `notContains`, `in`, or `notIn`.
- `value_json` (String) A JSON-encoded condition value. The `in` and `notIn` operators require a JSON array; the other operators require a scalar JSON value.

<a id="nestedatt--grants"></a>
### Nested Schema for `grants`

Optional:

- `all_scopes` (Boolean) Whether this grant includes all scopes.
- `allowed_audiences` (List of String) The audiences allowed by this grant.
- `scopes` (List of String) The scopes granted when this rule permits a request.

<a id="nestedatt--resource_targets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `resource_targets`

Required:

- `type` (String) The resource type matched by this target. Must be `api`, `mcp`, `outbound_app`, or `any`.

Optional:

- `all_of_type` (Boolean) Whether this target matches every resource of the selected type. This capability may require a project feature flag.
- `ids` (List of String) The specific resource IDs matched by this target when `all_of_type` is false.
